paper_title: On the difference between the Szeged and Wiener index
summary: Conjecture 5 from arXiv:1602.05184 strengthens the main result of the same paper (η(G) ≥ 2n−6 for 2-connected non-complete graphs) to η(G) ≥ 2n for n ≥ 10, excluding three specific graph families. A web search spanning 2016–2026 found no published paper that proves or disproves this stronger bound; the conjecture appears to remain open. Confidence is medium rather than high because the conjecture is now nine years old and absence of a resolution in the indexed literature is somewhat suspicious for a concretely stated bound.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper is 'Cops and Robber on Hyperbolic Manifolds' by Iršič, Mohar, Wesolek — completely unrelated to the Szeged–Wiener index. The fuzzy match (fuzz=80) was a false positive; this paper does not cite arXiv:1602.05184 nor discuss η(G).

search_queries: Szeged Wiener index difference eta 2-connected graph 2n conjecture Bonamy Knor
arXiv 1602.05184 Szeged Wiener index cite follow-up
Szeged Wiener index "eta(G)" OR "Sz(G)-W(G)" "2n" 2-connected conjecture solved proved 2017 2018 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024
notes: The source paper itself proves the weaker bound η(G) ≥ 2n−6 (resolving a conjecture of Nadjafi-Arani, Khodashenas and Ashrafi) and published Conjecture 5 as a further challenge. A 2021 paper (arXiv:2108.04157) resolves different conjectures of Hriňáková, Knor and Škrekovski (2019) about variable Wiener/Szeged indices, and a 2023 survey (arXiv:2303.11405) covers Wiener index topics, but neither could be verified as addressing Conjecture 5 specifically. No resolution of the full η(G) ≥ 2n statement was found.
